    Home › Aircraft › Piper PA-28 Archer

    Piper PA-28 Archer

    Piper · Light aircraft · In production — Piper Aircraft

    The low-wing trainer.

    Length7.32 m
    Wingspan10.8 m
    Range924 km
    MTOW1 t
    Typical seats4
    Built32,000+
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by aircraft.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Seats per metre#32 of 35
    0.55 seats/m typical seats ÷ fuselage length
    Range per tonne#4 of 65
    798.6 km/t maximum range ÷ MTOW in tonnes
    Span-to-length ratio#3 of 68
    1.48 wingspan ÷ fuselage length
    Weight per metre#67 of 68
    0.16 t/m MTOW ÷ fuselage length
    Range in hours#44 of 65
    4.0 h maximum range ÷ cruise speed
    Weight per seat#35 of 35
    289 kg MTOW ÷ typical seats

    Specification

    The numbers

    Length7.32 m
    Wingspan10.80 m
    Height2.20 m
    Max takeoff weight1,157 kg
    Cruise (75% power)232 km/h
    Range (55% power, 6,000 ft)924 km
    EngineLycoming O-360, 180 hp
    Service ceiling4,034 m
    Seats4

    Last verified: 2026-07-20 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    If the Skyhawk is the archetypal high-wing trainer, the Archer is the low-wing alternative, and the two have divided flight training between them for sixty years. More than 32,000 PA-28s of all types have been built since the Cherokee first flew in 1960.

    The low wing changes what a student learns. Ground effect is more pronounced on landing, so the flare has to be judged rather than simply allowed to happen. Visibility is better in a turn and worse straight down. Fuel feeds from tanks that sit below the cabin rather than above it, which means a fuel pump and a habit of checking it.

    The Archer uses a stabilator — a single all-moving horizontal surface — instead of a fixed tailplane with a hinged elevator. It is lighter in pitch than a Cessna and rewards a gentler hand. Prospective buyers should know the wing-spar airworthiness directive that applies to high-hour examples used in training.

    Common questions

    Piper PA-28 Archer — the short answers

    How fast is the Piper PA-28 Archer?
    The Piper PA-28 Archer cruises at about 232 km/h — roughly 125 knots, or 144 mph.
    How far can the Piper PA-28 Archer fly?
    The Piper PA-28 Archer has a maximum range of about 924 km (499 nautical miles, 574 miles). At its cruise speed that is roughly 4.0 hours in the air.
    How many people does the Piper PA-28 Archer carry?
    A typical Piper PA-28 Archer carries 4 people including crew. Exact figures vary with the operator's chosen configuration.
    Is the Piper PA-28 Archer still in production?
    Status: In production — Piper Aircraft. The Piper PA-28 Archer first flew in 1960, and about 32,000 have been built.
    How big is the Piper PA-28 Archer?
    The Piper PA-28 Archer is 7.32 m long with a wingspan of 10.8 m and a height of 2.2 m. Its maximum takeoff weight is 1,157 kg.
    Variants

    The Piper PA-28 Archer line

    PA-28-181 Archer III
    The current 180 hp production model, with the tapered wing introduced in the late 1970s.
    PA-28-161 Warrior
    The 160 hp sibling, widely used as a basic trainer where the extra 20 hp is not needed.
    PA-28-140 Cherokee
    The original two-seat trainer variant of the family, from 1964.
    PA-28R Arrow
    The retractable-gear version — the usual step-up aircraft for a complex endorsement.

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
